message:
feat(connection): add direct cursor insert path and incremental WAL refresh e2e

Major connection.rs refactoring (~400 new lines, ~280 removed):

- Add execute_prepared_direct_simple_insert_with_cursor for direct
  B-tree cursor insertion bypassing the full VDBE execution pipeline,
  importing PageReader + PageWriter + SharedTxnPageIo for typed access
- Extract default_statement_cache_capacity() helper replacing 8
  instances of NonZeroUsize::new(256).unwrap() with a safe const fn
- Add clippy::fn_params_excessive_bools allow for table program method

engine.rs: export SharedTxnPageIo type for connection-level cursor ops

New e2e test (bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2):
- bd_1dp9_6_7_8_2_incremental_wal_refresh.rs: 385-line test validating
  incremental WAL refresh with compiled cache invalidation
- verify script for manual reproduction

Also flushes beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

The result should tell future agents what is now justified, what remains speculative, and what was ruled out on evidence or correctness grounds.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:01.005881875Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:18:20.612189745Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","db300-program","db300-track-e","overlay-2026-03-23","ranking","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:26.154903402Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:25.584056811Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:01.005881875Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:53:30.708446333Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:23.866786041Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:24.436858812Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:24.995966629Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2578,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

This bead should produce the ranked frontier for what comes next, including approved cards, rejected cards, and speculative ideas that remain below threshold. Future agents should be able to pick up from this bead without reopening the full design debate.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:47:15Z"},{"id":2668,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"## E8.5 Advanced Card Verdicts and Ranked Frontier

Based on E8.1 decision record (settled from A7.1/A7.2/A7.3 evidence).

### Approved — Start Now

| Rank | Card | Target | EV | Verdict | Rationale |
|------|------|--------|-----|---------|-----------|
| **1** | **C8.6** | 8t flusher batch churn | 3.0 | **GO** | Narrowest scope, lowest risk. Flusher service time (30µs avg, 102µs spike) is the multiplier on waiter queue depth. Shrinking it proportionally reduces the 45% queueing bucket. |
| **2** | **D5.2** | c1 fused prepared-DML | 4.0 | **GO** | Independent code surface (connection.rs), parallel-safe with C8.6. Attacks the 70% ceremony-dominated c1 cell. Highest absolute EV. |

### Approved — Conditional (after C8.6 evidence)

| Rank | Card | Target | EV | Verdict | Gate |
|------|------|--------|-----|---------|------|
| **3** | **C8.4** | 8t/16t parallel WAL commit | 5.0 | **CONDITIONAL GO** | Start only after C8.6 lands AND post-C8.6 phase pack shows flusher service is no longer >50% of phase_B. If queueing is still dominant after C8.6, C8.4 is the only structural fix. |

### Deferred — Below Threshold

| Card | Target | EV | Verdict | Re-entry condition |
|------|--------|-----|---------|-------------------|
| C8.3 | 16t convoy audit | 1.5 | **DEFER** | Re-evaluate after C8.4 changes the flusher/waiter shape. The 22% timeout-wakes may vanish once the single-flusher monopoly is broken. |
| E3.3 | Snapshot publication | 1.5 | **DEFER** | Partially implemented. Pager Mutex is 5% of c1 cost. Complete after D5.2 proves the ceremony-elimination thesis. |
| DRO (bd-3t52f) | SSI abort policy | 1.0 | **DEFER** | Conflicts are <10% of cost in every red cell. Re-evaluate only if C8.4 creates conflict-dominated cells at higher concurrency. |
| C8.5 | Arrival-wait policy | 0.8 | **DEFER** | arrival_wait is 15µs (8t) and 2µs (16t). Not the dominant cost. Changing the heuristic before reducing flusher service risks making spikes worse. |

### Rejected

| Card | Verdict | Reason |
|------|---------|--------|
| HTM (bd-77l3t) | **REJECT** | Workspace forbids unsafe code. Flat combiner lock is <5% of any red cell. Design document preserved for reference. |
| io_uring | **REJECT** | wal_sync=0µs across all measured iterations. Raw I/O is not the bottleneck. |
| MVCC-conflict-first | **REJECT (anti-goal)** | MVCC/SW ratio is 0.924x at c1. Conflict work helps c8+/hot-page but is irrelevant to the two worst red cells (c1 ceremony, 8t queueing). |

### Frontier Summary

```
ACTIVE:  C8.6 ──┐
                 ├──▶ measure ──▶ C8.4 (if queueing still dominant)
ACTIVE:  D5.2 ──┘
WAITING: C8.3 (after C8.4)
WAITING: E3.3 (after D5.2)
WAITING: DRO  (after C8.4 if conflicts emerge)
DEAD:    HTM, io_uring, MVCC-conflict-first
```

### Note on incomplete dependencies
E8.2 (publication primitives), E8.3 (segmented durability), E8.4 (adaptive batching safety), C8.8 (post-step reruns), and D5.7 (c1 rerun) are still open. This verdict is based on E8.1's evidence-settled cards. If E8.2-E8.4 produce new candidates that pass the go/no-go rubric, they enter the frontier at their scored rank. The current frontier does not block on those beads — C8.6 and D5.2 are already approved and actionable.

 {"id":"bd-db300.6","title":"Track F: async pre-JIT and compiled-cache strategy","description":"## Goal
This track handles the user's pre-JIT hypothesis rigorously instead of hand-waving. The current JIT scaffold was pure overhead because it hashed programs and touched shared state on the hot path without ever producing a compiled fast path. A future compiled cache is only justified if statement repetition and compile amortization data show that interpreter-first plus asynchronous compilation can deliver net wins without contaminating latency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The project has a clear go/no-go decision on background pre-JIT.
- Any implementation keeps compile work off the execute path.
- Correctness, cache invalidation, and latency effects are explicitly tested.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","design":"- Decide based on measured reuse patterns, not intuition.
- Keep the hot path interpreter-first and non-blocking.
- If a compiled cache exists, compilation must happen off-path with clear invalidation and size controls.","acceptance_criteria":"- The project has a clear go/no-go decision on background pre-JIT.
- Any implementation keeps compile work off the execute path.
- Correctness, cache invalidation, and latency effects are explicitly tested.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"This track is intentionally lower priority than the commit-path and synchronization tracks. It is a leverage bet, not the current root cause, and it must stay evidence-driven.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:12.443426654Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:16.166627157Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["db300-program","db300-track-f","optimization","perf","prepared-statements","program","track-f","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.6.1","title":"F1: measure repeat-statement locality and compile amortization","description":"## Goal
Measure statement repetition, locality, and compile amortization so the project can decide whether an async pre-JIT path is actually worth building. This bead exists to keep the JIT idea evidence-driven instead of letting it become an attractive but unproven side quest, and it must account for whether reuse is lane-local or cross-core enough to justify background compilation without hurting front-end locality.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The workload data shows how often statements repeat, what the reuse distance looks like, how much locality is lane-local versus cross-core or cross-node, and what compile cost would need to be amortized.
2. First-hit latency, warmed-hit latency, and any measurable background-compile interference on hot benchmark cells are captured.
3. The result is a clear go or no-go recommendation for background compilation, including whether any compiled cache should be per-lane, shared, or rejected.
4. The recommendation is grounded in the real benchmark fixtures and at least one nontrivial placement profile, not a toy microbenchmark alone.
5. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The workload data shows how often statements repeat, what the reuse distance looks like, how much locality is lane-local versus cross-core or cross-node, and what compile cost would need to be amortized.
2. First-hit latency, warmed-hit latency, and any measurable background-compile interference on hot benchmark cells are captured.
3. The result is a clear go or no-go recommendation for background compilation, including whether any compiled cache should be per-lane, shared, or rejected.
4. The recommendation is grounded in the real benchmark fixtures and at least one nontrivial placement profile, not a toy microbenchmark alone.
5. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"Because the current JIT scaffold was all overhead and no compiled fast path, this bead must set a high bar for adding complexity back in. A cache that only helps under one lucky placement profile is not strong enough evidence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:06:43.970745425Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:16.371169616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-f","evidence","perf","prepared-statements","track-f","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.6.2","title":"F2: design an interpreter-first async compile queue with zero execute-path blocking","description":"## Goal
Design the interpreter-first async compile queue and compiled-cache lifecycle so compile work can happen off the execute path without polluting latency-sensitive lanes, instruction cache, LLC locality, or TLB behavior. This bead should turn the hybrid idea into a concrete design with compile-debt budgeting, duplicate suppression, admission control, and helper-core placement instead of a vague background-JIT concept.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The design states when a statement is eligible for background compilation and what locality or reuse evidence justifies spending cycles on it.
2. Compilation never blocks the current execution of the statement and runs under an explicit bounded queue or compile-debt budget tied to the many-core scheduling policy.
3. Cache fill, invalidation, duplicate-compile suppression, eviction, and concurrency behavior are explicit.
4. The design explains how compile work is placed to avoid harming foreground lanes, including whether it may share a core, LLC domain, or NUMA node with latency-sensitive execution.
5. The design defines metrics for first-hit latency, warmed-hit latency, compile success rate, queue depth, and background interference.
6. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The design states when a statement is eligible for background compilation and what locality or reuse evidence justifies spending cycles on it.
2. Compilation never blocks the current execution of the statement and runs under an explicit bounded queue or compile-debt budget tied to the many-core scheduling policy.
3. Cache fill, invalidation, duplicate-compile suppression, eviction, and concurrency behavior are explicit.
4. The design explains how compile work is placed to avoid harming foreground lanes, including whether it may share a core, LLC domain, or NUMA node with latency-sensitive execution.
5. The design defines metrics for first-hit latency, warmed-hit latency, compile success rate, queue depth, and background interference.
6. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","notes":"This design should assume the interpreter remains the fallback and initial execution path. The compiled cache is an optimization layer, not a new mandatory dependency, and it must not quietly trade away responsiveness for possible future wins.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:07:38.715479949Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:16.570381115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","db300-program","db300-track-f","parallelism","prepared-statements","track-f","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
